title: phoebe grandis
intro: Phoebe grandis, also known as Huang Pu Miao or Huang Pu Ma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae, native to Southeast Asia. The bark and roots of the Phoebe grandis plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as being used to treat respiratory and cardiovascular problems. Phoebe grandis is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the bark and roots being used to make tea or added to soups and other herbal preparations.
